title: Trace dependencies with bazel query
description: A procedure to inspect the real dependency graph and explain why one target depends on another.
body: Use when a rebuild is larger than expected or a dependency edge is surprising. Do not guess the graph from reading BUILD files alone. Steps: run bazel query to list a target's deps; use somepath or allpaths to find why target A reaches target B; use rdeps to find reverse dependents before a refactor. Validation signals include the query output matching the intended edges and explaining the rebuild scope. Risk boundary: refactoring without checking rdeps can silently break distant consumers, so map reverse dependents first.
